concerns.text: FR-003 mandates simulating for '[deferred] time steps' but no task exists to resolve this value or implement the logic to determine it (e.g., a feasibility study task). The plan notes this is deferred to Phase 0, but the tasks.md lacks a corresponding task to execute this resolution before simulation (T020-T025) proceeds.

FR-008 requires the system to 'explicitly define the statistical model... and justify the choice of multiple-comparison correction'. While T029 implements the correction logic, no task exists to generate the documentation or report section that explicitly defines and justifies the chosen model (single regression vs. multiple tests) as required by the spec.

SC-001 requires measuring sample variance of order parameter R across 1000 runs per topology to ensure stability (threshold 0.01). The tasks only cover 50 instances (T015) and do not include a task to perform the 1000-run stability check or the associated variance calculation and reporting.

SC-003 mandates measuring computational runtime against a ≤ 6-hour constraint. There is no task to instrument the code for runtime measurement, log the total execution time, or verify this constraint is met in the final report.

SC-004 requires verifying that the variation in headline correlation rate is ≤ 5% across the sensitivity sweep. While T030 implements the sweep, no task exists to calculate the specific variation metric and verify it meets the ≤ 5% threshold defined in the success criteria.

Phase 6 tasks (T033-T037) are tagged with [US3-Verify] or [US3], but FR-009 (which mandates this verification) is a distinct functional requirement not mapped to US3 in the spec. The tagging implies this is part of US3's scope, whereas the spec treats it as a separate verification requirement. This creates a traceability mismatch.
